cases.title: xFrontend — pageContext Homepage Slug Resolution
cases.notes: Store metadata populated (B2B-store, catalog fc596540864a41bf8ab78734ee7353a3). slugInfo.entityInfo null for empty permalink — acceptable.

cases.title: xFrontend — pageContext CMS Permalink Resolution
cases.notes: /search resolves with slugInfo structure present (entityInfo null — pure route, acceptable per test plan).

cases.title: xFrontend — pageContext Store Settings in Response
xFrontend — pageContext Authenticated vs Anonymous User Context
cases.notes: Anonymous vs ORG_USER context differs correctly. Auth user id=user-acme-store-maintainer-1, contact.firstName=François.

cases.title: xFrontend — pageContext Invalid Permalink
cases.notes: Bogus permalink returns HTTP 200 with slugInfo.entityInfo=null. No 500 / stack trace. Graceful degradation confirmed (BL-GQL-001).

cases.title: xFrontend — pageContext Organization-Scoped White Labeling
cases.notes: ORG_ID=organization-acme-store captured from me query. whiteLabelingSettings schema resolves: storeId=B2B-store, themePresetName=Coffee, logos null (org-specific WL not configured — acceptable per test plan).

notes: All 6 cases executed sequentially via scripts/graphql-runner.ts (runner-native, no browser).
ORG_USER auth token acquired successfully for GQL-055 and GQL-057 (auth latency ~550-630ms, well within thresholds).
GraphQL response times: 186-1036ms — all sub-second except the first anonymous call (cold cache).
No schema drift detected against knowledge/graphql-schema.md — all fields (pageContext, store, slugInfo, user, contact, whiteLabelingSettings) resolved as documented.
BL-GQL-001 (no HTTP 500 on invalid input) and BL-GQL-002 (store metadata invariants) verified. BL-B2B-001 (org-scoped query) and BL-GQL-004 (auth context differentiation) verified.
